instructions.instruction: Use `vault-cli capture --text "<content>"` to save information to persistent memory
instructions.relevant_when: User confirms a decision, preference, constraint, or asks to save something
instructions.why_given: The agent must know the exact CLI invocation to capture memories

instructions.instruction: Use `--tier semantic` when capturing distilled facts or rules (not time-bound events)
instructions.relevant_when: Capturing a principle, fact, convention, or rule that should persist long-term
instructions.why_given: Tier selection affects memory durability and how memories are later consolidated

instructions.instruction: Use `--tier procedural` when capturing how-to processes or step-by-step instructions
instructions.relevant_when: Capturing a workflow, process, or set of steps the user wants remembered
instructions.why_given: Procedural memories are permanent until explicitly revoked and get special handling

instructions.instruction: Use `--project <id>` to scope a memory to a specific project
instructions.relevant_when: User is working in a project context and the memory is project-specific
instructions.why_given: Without a project scope, the memory is global and may pollute other project contexts

instructions.instruction: Use `--tags` to attach relevant keywords for later retrieval
instructions.relevant_when: The memory content has clear topic keywords that aid future search
instructions.why_given: Tags improve BM25 search quality for this memory

instructions.instruction: Capture on confirmed decisions, constraints, recurring bugs, naming conventions, and user preferences
original_snippets: architectural decisions, recurring bugs, hard-won constraints, naming conventions, or user preferences
instructions.relevant_when: The conversation produces something worth remembering across sessions
instructions.why_given: The agent must recognise the right moments to invoke capture proactively

instructions.instruction: Default tier is episodic; only override when the content is clearly a fact/rule or a procedure
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding which tier to assign to a capture
instructions.why_given: Using the wrong tier causes incorrect memory handling during consolidation
